name: Willis Wall
description: Willis Wall is a Grade V mixed alpine route rated as one of the most fearsome alpine faces in the lower 48 states. Between Liberty Ridge and Curtis Ridge on Mount Rainier's north face. The wall features continuous mixed climbing on steep slopes between 45-60 degrees, with sustained rock and ice sections.
approach: Access via Liberty Ridge approach. Reach White River, follow to Glacier Basin Trail (~3.5 mi). Continue to base of Liberty Ridge.

name: Central Mowich Face
description: Central Mowich Face offers two route variations on the northwest face of Mount Rainier. Route follows steep snow and ice sections on a north-facing glacier. Two main variations exist: Grade III (direct, snow-dominant) and Grade IV (ice-steep variation).
approach: Wonderland Trail from Mowich Lake for 4 miles to Spray Park. Climbers trail ascends lower Ptarmigan Ridge. At 8,300 ft, descend 1,000 ft to North Mowich Glacier.

name: North Mowich Headwall (Edmunds Headwall)
description: North Mowich Headwall (Edmunds Headwall) is a steep Grade IV ice and mixed route on the north side of Mount Rainier. Features sustained steep slopes and narrow hourglass rock/ice section.
approach: Wonderland Trail to Spray Park (4 miles). Climbers trail up lower Ptarmigan Ridge. At 8,300 ft, descend 1,000 ft to North Mowich Glacier. High camp placed ~10,000 ft.

name: Avalanche Glacier
description: Avalanche Glacier is a steeper alternative to the popular South Spur route on Mount Adams. Ascends directly up a glacier with moderate ice climbing sections. More direct approach to summit than South Spur.
approach: From Cold Springs trailhead, traverse via round-the-mountain trail to the northwest side of Mount Adams, approach glacier directly from southwest.

name: Northwest Ridge (North Face of Northwest Ridge)
description: Northwest Ridge is an intermediate Grade II ice and snow route on the north side of Mount Adams. More aesthetic than South Spur with less crowding. Moderate snow and ice climbing with good views.
approach: Forest Service Road 2329 from Taklakh Lake area (5.7 miles). Killen Creek Trail (#113) to Pacific Crest Trail junction, then High Camp Trail (#10) to meadow camp at 6,900 ft.

name: West Ridge
description: West Ridge is probably the second most popular technical route on Mount Stuart after the North Ridge. Grade II, 5.4 alpine rock climbing with scrambling. Scenic and well-protected with varied climbing.
approach: I-90 exit 85 to SR-970 (6.6 mi), left on Teanaway River Road (23 mi to road end). Trail to Ingalls Pass (6,480 ft), continue to Ingalls Lake. Short of Stuart Pass in upper meadows of Ingalls Creek, ascend to base of West Ridge.

name: Cascadian Couloir
description: Cascadian Couloir is the standard non-technical route on Mount Stuart and probably the most climbed route on the peak. Over 4,600 feet of Class 3 rock scrambling via the southeast flank to false summit (9,200 ft), then Class 2-3 scrambling east to the main summit.
approach: Esmeralda Trail (old jeep road ~0.5 miles), right at fork to Ingalls Way Trail. At Long's Pass Trail/Ingalls Pass Trail junction, right to Long's Pass (6,300 ft). Continue to route start near Ingalls Lake.
